title: Superpowers Sample Spec Package
summary: Evidence produced by the Superpowers lifecycle for a Databricks ingestion pipeline sample project, showing how each stage turns a vague coding-agent request into governed spec-driven delivery.

sections.title: Discovery brief
sections.summary: Brainstorm / Design lifecycle evidence captures users, goals, non-goals, and risks before the sample Databricks work begins.
items: Primary users are finance analysts, merchandising leads, store operations, and customer support supervisors.
The Data Lakehouse scope covers bronze receipt ingestion, silver normalization, gold KPI marts, Databricks SQL dashboards, and Lakebase lookup extracts.
Delta Lake is required for receipt replay, quarantine recovery, and auditable updates after late returns or tax corrections.
Unity Catalog must protect customer identifiers and payment-adjacent attributes before broad dashboard rollout.
Non-goal: replacing the point-of-sale transaction system in this release.

sections.title: Formal specification excerpt
sections.summary: Specification lifecycle evidence defines observable contracts for ingestion, governance, analytics, and serving boundaries.
items: Bronze Delta Lake tables accept receipt payloads with source, receipt id, event timestamp, raw text, and ingestion metadata.
Silver tables reject records missing receipt id, store id, transaction timestamp, or total amount, and write rejected rows to a quarantine table with reason codes.
Unity Catalog tags customer name, loyalty id, email, phone, and tender reference fields; analyst roles receive masked values unless explicitly approved.
Databricks SQL dashboards read only gold tables and expose daily sales, tax variance, return rate, and promotion leakage metrics.
Lakebase receives only curated receipt lookup fields and must show synchronization age so support teams know whether a receipt is current.

sections.title: Implementation plan excerpt
sections.summary: Implementation Planning lifecycle evidence splits the spec into ordered, testable increments before execution starts.
items: Task 1: create Delta Lake bronze and silver schemas plus fixture receipts for valid, malformed, late, and returned transactions.
Task 2: implement receipt normalization and quarantine rules with unit tests for schema drift and missing required fields.
Task 3: register Unity Catalog tables, tags, grants, and lineage checks for restricted receipt attributes.
Task 4: publish Databricks SQL gold marts and dashboard definitions against governed tables only.
Task 5: prepare Lakebase receipt lookup synchronization with freshness checks and failure reporting.

sections.title: TDD matrix
sections.summary: Test-Driven Development lifecycle evidence maps tests to promised behavior instead of incidental implementation details.
items: Given a valid point-of-sale receipt, bronze ingestion writes the raw payload and silver normalization emits one receipt header plus line items in Delta Lake.
Given a receipt missing total amount, silver normalization writes no business row and records a quarantine reason.
Given a support analyst role, Unity Catalog policy allows Lakebase lookup fields but masks restricted PII columns in exploratory queries.
Given corrected tax data, Databricks SQL gold marts recompute tax variance without double-counting the original receipt.
Given a Lakebase synchronization failure, the status endpoint reports stale data instead of implying that receipt lookup is current.

sections.title: Debug log narrative
sections.summary: Systematic Debugging lifecycle evidence records observations, hypotheses, experiments, and the proven root cause.
items: Symptom: Databricks SQL tax variance spiked after a store firmware rollout even though point-of-sale control totals were stable.
First observation: the Delta Lake quarantine table grew for receipts where tax was renamed from tax_total to taxes.total.
Hypothesis: schema drift was handled in bronze but not mapped in silver normalization.
Experiment: replayed one affected receipt through the parser and confirmed silver dropped tax while preserving gross total.
Fix: added an explicit schema evolution mapping and a regression test that fails if tax fields are silently omitted.

sections.title: Review findings and dispositions
sections.summary: Code Review lifecycle evidence turns findings into concrete dispositions rather than vague agreement.
items: Finding: Unity Catalog grants allowed a merchandising group to read loyalty identifiers in silver tables. Disposition: removed direct silver access and routed the group to masked gold views.
Finding: Lakebase lookup did not expose synchronization age. Disposition: added last_successful_sync_at and stale_after_minutes to the serving contract.
Finding: Databricks SQL dashboard definitions referenced a developer schema. Disposition: changed all queries to catalog-qualified production gold views.
Finding: Delta Lake quarantine retention was undefined. Disposition: set 180-day retention for audit review and replay.

sections.title: Verification checklist and sign-off criteria
sections.summary: Verification and Branch Completion lifecycle evidence requires fresh proof before completion claims or handoff.
items: All receipt normalization tests pass against valid, malformed, late, returned, and schema-drift fixtures.
Delta Lake table history confirms controlled updates and replay for corrected receipts.
Unity Catalog lineage shows source receipt tables feeding silver, gold, Databricks SQL dashboards, and Lakebase serving extracts.
Finance validates Databricks SQL gross sales, taxes, discounts, and returns against control totals for two closed business days.
Support validates Lakebase receipt lookup freshness and stale-state messaging before operational sign-off.
